activities_projects.title: Garden Feature Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand different garden features and their purposes
steps: Look at the list of garden items given in the textbook
Discuss in groups the type of garden you would like to have
List features such as ponds, flower beds, and hedges
Explain the reasons for choosing those features
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to explain ideas about garden design and nature
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of ideas and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Meaning of the Title
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the literal and symbolic meaning of the poem's title
steps: Read the title 'A Concrete Example'
Discuss the literal meaning of 'concrete'
Discuss the symbolic meaning in the context of the poem
Write a short explanation in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how titles reflect deeper meaning in literature

activities_projects.title: Poem Structure Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the structure and rhyme scheme of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the rhyme scheme in each stanza
Write the rhyme pattern in your notebook
Discuss how rhyme creates rhythm in poetry
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of rhyme scheme (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Study: Mrs. Jones
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the personality of Mrs. Jones based on the poem
steps: Read the poem and identify descriptions of Mrs. Jones
Write the qualities that describe her personality
Support your answers with lines from the poem
Share your observations with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to infer character traits from literary descriptions

activities_projects.title: Imagery Identification
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify lines that create a visual image of the garden
Write these lines in your notebook
Explain what picture comes to your mind while reading them
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how imagery helps visualise scenes in poetry
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of examples and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Irony in the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Understand the use of irony in literature
steps: Read the poem again carefully
Identify the situation where the speaker stands on the flower
Explain why this situation is ironic
Discuss how it adds humour to the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand situational irony in literature

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Expansion
steps: Find words such as 'peculiar', 'delicate', and 'lovely'
Use a dictionary to find meanings
Write synonyms and create sentences using those words
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop vocabulary and contextual understanding

activities_projects.title: Word Formation Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand how words change by replacing letters
steps: Look at the example word 'nice'
Replace the first letter to create new words
Match the new words with the given hints
Write the correct words in your notebook

activities_projects.title: Listening Activity on Rock Garden
steps: Listen carefully to the presentation about the Rock Garden of Chandigarh
Write answers to the questions based on what you hear
Discuss the answers with classmates

activities_projects.title: Formal and Informal Apology Practice
steps: Read the apology situations given in the textbook
Practice apologising in both informal and formal situations
Work with a partner to create short dialogues
Present your dialogue to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn appropriate language for different situations
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and correctness of communication (10 marks)
